title: Why every startup should be an AI company now
post:
The traditional startup playbook — hire slow, build big teams, raise capital for headcount — is being rewritten. Companies that embrace AI as a core operational principle are shipping faster, iterating quicker, and spending a fraction of what their predecessors did.

I'm not saying every startup should chase the latest AI hype. I'm saying the cost structure and speed advantages are so significant that ignoring them is now a competitive risk. The tools exist. The talent is available. The only question is whether you're willing to rethink how your company actually works.

What's holding you back from building an AI-first company? Thoughts?
